sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The Notification class, implemented in Cython, provides a simple wrapper around an underlying C++ notification object within the AnalysisG framework. Its primary purpose is to facilitate communication and logging, offering a Python-friendly interface to C++ notification functionalities. This class is designed to be lightweight and efficient, enabling seamless integration of C++-level notifications into Python-based workflows.

sect1.title: The Notification Class
sect1.para: Notification is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. This allows for direct and optimized interaction with the C++ notification object. It encapsulates a pointer (self.ptr) to a C++ notification instance, managing its lifecycle.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It is intentionally left empty, as the C++ object is initialized in __init__.
__init__(self): The Python initializer. It creates a new C++ notification object and assigns its pointer to self.ptr.
__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It deallocates the C++ notification object pointed to by self.ptr, ensuring proper memory management.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The Notification class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class Notification: Declares Notification as a Cython extension type.
cdef notification *ptr: A C++ pointer to a notification object, managed by the Cython class.
from AnalysisG.core.notification cimport notification: Imports the C++ notification class declaration from its .pxd file.
